cards.back: An Elasticsearch shell is a command-line interface for interacting with an Elasticsearch cluster

cards.front: What is the purpose of an ES shell?
cards.back: To execute Elasticsearch commands, query data, and manage the cluster

cards.front: What is the main difference between the ES shell and Kibana?
cards.back: The ES shell is a command-line interface for Elasticsearch, while Kibana is a visualization interface

cards.front: What are some common use cases for the ES shell?
cards.back: Data exploration, ad-hoc queries, and data manipulation before sending to a frontend application

cards.front: What are some popular ES shell commands?
cards.back: GET, GET [index], GET [index]/[type], PUT, DELETE, HEAD, and the _search API
